rationale.R1.6: "Clean this up" applied to a prose artifact is canonically a writing-polish request — sentence clarity, concision, flow, word choice, and paragraph cohesion are the load-bearing deliverables; failure to improve prose quality directly invalidates the delegation.
rationale.R1.2: Peripheral: editing may surface and correct minor internal inconsistencies (e.g., the related-work framing vs. the positioning claim), but logical audit is not the stated goal.
rationale.R1.5: Peripheral: light reordering within a paragraph is in scope for polish, but wholesale structural reorganization is not implied by "clean this up."
rationale.R1.7: Peripheral: citation-style tidying (parenthetical formatting, author–year consistency) is a natural side-effect of prose cleanup but is not a primary responsibility.
